commit 80f51574c94d2e56fa9d2f5093f4b9e558da4b67
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Sat May 2 21:41:38 2026 -0400

    fix(agent): emit picker.priority_skip + claim_race diagnostic events [ddx-9d55601f]
    
    The execute-loop picker (Store.ReadyExecution -> nextCandidate) was
    correct: it sorts by priority asc and never reorders. The "P0 starvation"
    operators reported in the field was actually the four "ready" P0 beads
    sitting on extra.execute-loop-retry-after — correctly excluded by
    readyFiltered. The bug was that there was no observability surface to
    explain *why* a worker passed over a higher-priority bead, so an
    operator who saw `ddx bead ready` show a P0 had no way to tell whether
    the picker was broken, the bead was upstream-filtered, or a label
    filter was in play.
    
    This adds two structured events to the loop event sink:
    
    - picker.priority_skip: fired when nextCandidate selects a bead at
      priority N while at least one strictly higher-priority bead (< N)
      was passed over. Each skipped bead names its reason (in_attempted,
      label_filter); eligibility_filter and retry_cooldown are reserved
      in the vocabulary for future re-emit if those filters move into
      nextCandidate.
    - picker.claim_race: fired when Store.Claim fails (another worker
      grabbed the bead under the WithLock atomic). Names the bead,
      priority, and the underlying error so concurrent-worker losses
      are observable.
    
    Also adds three regression tests:
    - TestExecuteLoop_ClaimsHighestPriorityFirst (AC #2)
    - TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s (AC #3)
    - TestExecuteLoop_EmitsPickerPrioritySkipEvent (AC #4)
    
    Full investigation in
    .ddx/executions/20260502T231414-92c45184/picker-priority-bug.md.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

80f51574 fix(agent): emit picker.priority_skip + claim_race diagnostic events [ddx-9d55601f]

1	# Picker priority "starvation" — investigation (ddx-9d55601f)
2	
3	## TL;DR
4	
5	The picker is **not** violating priority ordering. The four "starved" P0
6	beads in the operator's reproducer were all parked on
7	`extra.execute-loop-retry-after` at a future timestamp, so they were
8	correctly excluded from `Store.ReadyExecution()` (the source-of-truth
9	queue used by `ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate`). The bead description's
10	claim that `extra.execute-loop-retry-after: null` for ddx-dc157075,
11	ddx-aee651ec, and ddx-29058e2a was incorrect at observation time.
12	
13	What we did fix: emitted the missing diagnostic event so the **next**
14	operator who sees this won't have to spend a session diffing call
15	graphs to find out why.
16	
17	## Code path traced (per AC #1)
18	
19	```
20	ExecuteBeadWorker.Run(...)                               // execute_bead_loop.go:349
21	  ↳ nextCandidate(attempted, labelFilter)                // execute_bead_loop.go:1146
22	      ↳ Store.ReadyExecution()                           // bead/store.go:1246
23	          ↳ readyFiltered(executionOnly=true)            // bead/store.go:1301
24	              · status==open || stalled-in-progress
25	              · all deps closed
26	              · execution-eligible != false              // line 1334
27	              · superseded-by empty                       // line 1341
28	              · execute-loop-retry-after in past          // line 1346  ← excludes parked P0s
29	              · sortBeadsForQueue(ready)                  // line 1357 → priority asc, then created_at asc, then ID asc
30	      · loop: skip if in attempted map                    // line 1170
31	      · loop: skip if labelFilter set and label missing   // line 1175
32	      · return first survivor
33	  ↳ PreClaimHook  // continue on err (one retry)
34	  ↳ attempted[id] = struct{}{}                            // execute_bead_loop.go:480 (BEFORE Claim, intentional — see below)
35	  ↳ RoutePreflight  // return on err
36	  ↳ ComplexityGate  // continue on skip / re-pick
37	  ↳ Store.Claim(id, assignee)  // atomic via WithLock
38	      · if Claim fails (race lost) → continue → re-pick from ReadyExecution
39	```
40	
41	The sort comparator at `bead/store.go:1473`:
42	
43	```go
44	if beads[i].Priority != beads[j].Priority {
45	    return beads[i].Priority < beads[j].Priority   // P0 first ✓
46	}
47	```
48	
49	There is no path in either `nextCandidate` or `ReadyExecution` that
50	reorders or down-ranks priority. Every `continue` in `nextCandidate`
51	preserves the priority ordering of the survivors.
52	
53	## The five hypotheses, evaluated
54	
55	1. **`attempted` map persists across worker restarts.** No. It is allocated
56	   per `Run()` call at `execute_bead_loop.go:382` (`attempted := make(map[string]struct{})`).
57	   A "fresh" worker process gets an empty map. **Rejected.**
58	
59	2. **Empty label filter excludes labels.** No. The guard at
60	   `execute_bead_loop.go:1175` is `labelFilter != "" && !HasBeadLabel(...)`.
61	   Empty filter is a no-op. **Rejected.**
62	
63	3. **Two workers race on `Claim()` and the loser falls through to lower priority.**
64	   *Half true.* The loser does call `continue`, but on the next iteration:
65	   - The contended bead is now `in_progress` (Claim flipped it under
66	     `WithLock`), so it is **not** in `ReadyExecution`'s output anymore.
67	   - The next ready P0 is at the head of the list, and the loser will
68	     pick **that** P0 — not a P2.
69	   - The bead also stays in the loser's `attempted` map (added pre-Claim
70	     at line 480), but that's harmless because the bead is no longer
71	     ready anyway. The map entry just makes the skip a tautology.
72	
73	   So while the **mechanism** exists (loser does `continue` after Claim
74	   fails), it does not produce the observed P2-claim-while-P0-ready
75	   behavior on its own. **Rejected as root cause** — but added a
76	   `picker.claim_race` diagnostic so future races are observable.
77	
78	4. **Server-managed worker uses a different picker.** No. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:775`
79	   calls `worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{...})` — the same
80	   `ExecuteBeadWorker.Run` driven by the CLI. There is no parallel
81	   selection path in `workers.go`. **Rejected.**
82	
83	5. **The P0 beads have a label/area excluded by some default filter.** No.
84	   `LabelFilter` is plumbed only from `spec.LabelFilter`
85	   (`cli/internal/server/workers.go:782`). No default value, no implicit
86	   filter applied anywhere. **Rejected.**
87	
88	## The actual cause of the operator's observation
89	
90	At the time of the reproducer (2026-05-02 23:46 UTC), `ddx bead show`
91	returned the following for the three "ready P0" beads named in the bead
92	description:
93	
94	```
95	=== ddx-dc157075 ===
96	  execute-loop-retry-after: 2026-05-03T02:53:53Z   (~3 hours in the future)
97	=== ddx-aee651ec ===
98	  execute-loop-retry-after: 2026-05-03T02:54:00Z
99	=== ddx-29058e2a ===
100	  execute-loop-retry-after: 2026-05-03T02:54:38Z
101	```
102	
103	`ddx bead ready` (used by the operator) shows beads whose dependencies are
104	satisfied, but **does not** filter by `execute-loop-retry-after`. The
105	execution queue (`ddx bead ready --execution`, equivalent to
106	`Store.ReadyExecution()`) **does** filter — and correctly returned the
107	three beads as cooldown-parked.
108	
109	So the workers were behaving correctly: the highest-priority *eligible*
110	bead was a P2 because every P0 in the queue was on retry cooldown.
111	
112	The bead description's statement
113	"`extra.execute-loop-retry-after: null`" was a snapshot from an earlier
114	state (or an inspection of a different field); the live state at
115	worker-claim time had cooldowns set.
116	
117	## What changed
118	
119	Even though the picker is correct, it had no observability surface for
120	"why didn't you pick this P0?" — exactly the question the operator was
121	trying to answer. AC #4 demands a diagnostic event when this happens. We
122	added two:
123	
124	1. `picker.priority_skip` — fired when `nextCandidate` selects a bead at
125	   priority N while at least one strictly higher-priority bead (`< N`)
126	   was passed over. Each skipped bead names its reason: `in_attempted`
127	   (already tried this run) or `label_filter` (worker spec restricted).
128	   `eligibility_filter` and `retry_cooldown` are reserved for future
129	   re-emit when those filters move into `nextCandidate` (currently they
130	   apply upstream in `ReadyExecution` and never reach `nextCandidate`).
131	
132	2. `picker.claim_race` — fired when `Store.Claim` fails (another worker
133	   grabbed the bead first). Names the bead, priority, and the underlying
134	   error.
135	
136	With these in place, an operator who sees a worker claim a P2 while
137	`ddx bead ready` shows a P0 can now run
138	`ddx server workers log <id> | grep picker.` and immediately see whether
139	the P0 is on cooldown (no skip event because the bead is upstream-filtered
140	— operator can then `ddx bead show <p0>` to find the cooldown), in
141	the worker's `attempted` map (skip event with reason=in_attempted —
142	indicates a no-changes loop that should be investigated), label-filtered
143	out (skip event with reason=label_filter), or actively contended
144	(claim_race event).
145	
146	## Files changed
147	
148	- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`
149	  - `nextCandidate` extended to return `[]pickerSkip` alongside the
150	    chosen bead so the caller can build the diagnostic event.
151	  - New helper `emitPickerPrioritySkips` emits `picker.priority_skip`
152	    only when a strictly higher-priority bead was passed over (a same-
153	    priority FIFO loss is not starvation).
154	  - Added `emit("picker.claim_race", ...)` at the Claim-failure branch.
155	- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_priority_test.go` (new):
156	  - `TestExecuteLoop_ClaimsHighestPriorityFirst` (AC #2)
157	  - `TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s` (AC #3)
158	  - `TestExecuteLoop_EmitsPickerPrioritySkipEvent` (AC #4)
159	
160	## What was deliberately NOT changed
161	
162	- The `attempted` map is still populated **before** `Claim`. Moving it
163	  to after `Claim` would create an infinite-pick loop on the
164	  ComplexityGate `shouldClaim=false` path, which currently relies on the
165	  pre-Claim `attempted` add to prevent re-picking the same coarse bead.
166	  The Claim-race "loser keeps bead in attempted" is harmless because
167	  the bead is no longer in `ReadyExecution` after the winner's Claim
168	  flipped its status under `WithLock`.
169	- The sort comparator was left untouched. Priority ordering is correct.
170	- No retry/cooldown logic was modified — the upstream filter in
171	  `readyFiltered` is correct; what was missing was operator visibility
172	  via `ddx bead ready` (out of scope for this bead; that command's
173	  filtering is a separate concern).
174	
175	## Manual verification (per AC #5)
176	
177	After commit, the next freshly-started worker will pick `ddx-9d55601f`
178	first (it has Priority=0, no cooldown, no label filter, all deps
179	closed) — assuming no race with another worker already mid-Claim.
180	ddx-dc157075, ddx-aee651ec, ddx-29058e2a will be picked when their
181	respective `execute-loop-retry-after` timestamps elapse.
182

input.content:
Bead ddx-9d55601f was already implemented and merged in commit 80f51574
("fix(agent): emit picker.priority_skip + claim_race diagnostic events
[ddx-9d55601f]"), which is reachable from the current HEAD (9cf40e4e).

Status of acceptance criteria against HEAD:

AC #1 — Root cause + investigation report.
  Report at .ddx/executions/20260502T231414-92c45184/picker-priority-bug.md
  (the previous attempt's run-id). Conclusion: the picker is not
  violating priority ordering; the operator's "starved" P0s were on
  extra.execute-loop-retry-after cooldown and correctly excluded by
  Store.ReadyExecution()/readyFiltered. Full call-graph trace and
  per-hypothesis evaluation included.

AC #2 — TestExecuteLoop_ClaimsHighestPriorityFirst exists at
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_priority_test.go.

AC #3 — TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s exists at
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_priority_test.go.

AC #4 — Two diagnostic events added in execute_bead_loop.go:
  - picker.priority_skip with reasons {in_attempted, label_filter}
    (eligibility_filter, retry_cooldown reserved in vocabulary; those
    filters apply upstream in readyFiltered and never reach
    nextCandidate, so re-emitting them there would be misleading).
  - picker.claim_race fired when Store.Claim loses to a concurrent
    worker. Covered by TestExecuteLoop_EmitsPickerPrioritySkipEvent.

AC #5 — Runtime/manual claim verification; cannot be exercised from
  this isolated worktree.

AC #6 — Verified locally:
  cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...
  -> ok github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent (85.208s)
  -> ok github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/escalation
  -> ok github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/testfixtures
  -> ok github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead

No further code changes are warranted on this attempt. The bead appears
to have been re-queued after the prior commit landed (no
<review-findings> in this attempt's prompt). Closing the bead — rather
than committing a redundant change — is the correct outcome.

data.text: No code changes this attempt. The bead was already implemented in commit 80f51574 (reachable from HEAD): diagnostic events `picker.priority_skip` and `picker.claim_race` in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`, three regression tests in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_priority_test.go`, and the investigation report at `.ddx/executions/20260502T231414-92c45184/picker-priority-bug.md`. Target tests pass green. Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` under this run-id explaining the re-queue.
